message:
fix(cli,server): surface serde errors instead of hand-rolled fallback JSON; simplify Windows rename branch

Same theme as the preceding share-crate audit: stop relying on
`unwrap_or_else(|_| format!("{{\"error\":\"{msg}\"}}"))` style manual
JSON fallbacks in error paths, because they silently corrupt output
whenever the detail message contains a quote or backslash.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/output.rs
---------------------------------------
* Replaces the two `serde_json::to_string[_pretty](data).unwrap_or_else(|_| "{}".to_string())`
  calls in `emit_output` (JSON and TOON branches) with three new
  helpers that surface the actual serialization error to the caller
  as a valid JSON document:
    - `encode_json_pretty_or_error<T: Serialize>(data) -> String` —
      pretty JSON encoding; on failure, falls through to
      `serialize_output_error_json(..., pretty=true)`.
    - `encode_json_compact_or_error<T: Serialize>(data) -> String` —
      compact JSON encoding used by the TOON pre-render; same
      fallback with `pretty=false`.
    - `serialize_output_error_json(message, pretty)` builds a
      `{ "error": message }` JSON payload via `serde_json::json!`
      and serialises it. The inner `to_string[_pretty]` call is
      expected to succeed (the payload is a trivial object) and
      panics with a clear message if it ever fails — far better
      than emitting malformed JSON.
* The TOON branch now uses the same error-aware encoder for its
  fallback path. Previously a failing TOON conversion would silently
  reach for `to_string_pretty(...).unwrap_or_else(|_| "{}")` and
  swallow the error.
* New unit test
  `emit_output_json_format_surfaces_serialization_errors` uses the
  same `FailingSerialize` stub pattern as the share crate to verify
  the JSON output path emits a structured `{"error": "output
  serialization failed: boom"}` instead of an empty object.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs
-------------------------------------------
* New `encode_json_error_body(field, detail) -> Result<String, (u16, String)>`
  helper encapsulates the `serde_json::json!({field: detail})` +
  `to_string` pattern and propagates any serialization error as an
  HTTP 500 with a "JSON error: ..." detail.
* `json_err` and `json_detail_err` now route through the helper
  instead of each hand-rolling its own
  `unwrap_or_else(|_| format!("{{\"error\":\"{detail}\"}}"))`
  fallback. Previously a detail containing `"` or `\n` would
  produce invalid JSON whenever serde actually failed (which does
  happen for pathological control characters).
* `parse_overseer_body`'s inline `err` closure gets the same
  treatment — it now match-s on `encode_json_error_body(...)` and
  forwards the original serializer error up to the HTTP layer.
* Two new tests verify the JSON escape behaviour:
    - `json_err_escapes_error_detail` — passes
      `"bad \"quote\"\nline"` through `json_err(422, ...)` and
      asserts the resulting body parses as JSON with `error` equal
      to the original detail.
    - `json_detail_err_escapes_detail_payload` — same for
      `json_detail_err(404, ...)` with a detail containing both
      quotes and a literal backslash.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_persist.rs
-----------------------------------------------
* Tidies the `atomic_write_text` Windows rename branch. The
  previous code caught a rename error, then inside `#[cfg(windows)]`
  checked whether the target existed and retried the rename; on
  non-Windows it just returned the original error. The new
  structure runs the `#[cfg(windows)]` pre-delete-and-retry first
  and then calls `std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, path)?` unconditionally,
  so the success/failure handling is the same on every platform and
  the error path is shorter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
